name: claude-hooks-context-token-budget
tags: token budget context memories trim cap ups dispatcher enforce_context_budget 4000 tiktoken task:3de05199
body:
UPS enforces a 4000-token budget (_CONTEXT_TOKEN_BUDGET in hooks/dispatcher.py) across the 4 task-activation context categories (memories, related_tasks, related_commits, task_rag_chunks) before formatting the system prompt. Only `memories` gets trimmed on overflow — popped from the tail, since load_memories.py already returns it sorted descending by score with no cap. The other 3 categories are pre-capped at top-3 each and left untouched.

Why: memories was the only uncapped category and thus the only realistic source of a budget blowout; related_tasks/related_commits/task_rag_chunks are already small.

How to apply: if UPS context still exceeds budget in practice (visible via the ctx_tokens(...) fields in the UPS done sqlite log line), extend trimming to the other categories rather than assuming a bigger memories cap will fix it — see task:3de05199 and task:3369b86a for the logging/budget implementation.
